div.p: William, by the grace of God abbot of Holyrood, and the convent of the same house, to all the faithful, Greeting: Let both future and present know that we have granted, and by this our charter made firm, to the lady Affrica, daughter of Edgar, our whole land that we have from the donation of her father in the territory of Dalgarnock, to be held of us for the whole of her life, by returning thereupon to us annually for the first four years one merk of silver, half at the feast of St Martin, and half at Pentecost, but after four years, at the same terms for the whole of her life, twenty shillings. If, however, the said land should be uncultivated or waste, the foresaid Affrica should pay us one silver merk at the forementioned terms for as long as that land should be waste or uncultivated. This agreement was made in the year of the Incarnation of the Lord 1221, and in testimony of this agreement we affixed our seal and the seal of the foresaid Affrica to the present document.
